question: 1. If you were to consider your life in general these days, how happy or unhappy would you say you are, on the whole?
2. In general, how much confidence do you have in the health care system in [country]?
3. Is it fair or unfair that people with higher incomes can afford better health care than people with lower incomes?
4a. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People use health care services more than necessary.
4b. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: The government should provide only limited health care services.
4c. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: In general, the health care system in [country] is inefficient.
5. How willing would you be to pay higher taxes to improve the level of health care for all people in [country]?
6a. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People should have access to publicly funded health care even if they do not hold citizenship of [country].
6b. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People should have access to publicly funded health care even if they behave in ways that damage their health.
7a. In [country], do you think it is easier or harder to get access to health care for rich people than for poor people?
7b. In [country], do you think it is easier or harder to get access to health care for old people than for young people?
7c. In [country], do you think it is easier or harder to get access to health care for women than for men?
7d. In [country], do you think it is easier or harder to get access to health care for citizens of [country] than for people who do not hold [country's] citizenship?

8a. Severe health problems may have many causes. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People suffer from severe health problems because they behaved in ways that damaged their health.

8b. Severe health problems may have many causes. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People suffer from severe health problems because of the environment they are exposed to at work or where they live.
8c. Severe health problems may have many causes. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People suffer from severe health problems because of their genes.
8d. Severe health problems may have many causes. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: People suffer from severe health problems because they are poor.

9. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: [Alternative/traditional/folk] medicine provides better solutions for health problems than [mainstream/Western conventional] medicine?
10a. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement about doctors in general in [country]: All things considered, doctors can be trusted.
10b. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement about doctors in general in [country]: The medical skills of doctors are not as good as they should be.
10c. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement about doctors in general in [country]: Doctors care more about their earnings than about their patients.
11. During the past 12 months, how often, if at all, did you use the internet on any device to look for health or medical information for yourself or someone else?
option_text: I do not have access to the internet

question: 12a. During the past 12 months, how often, if at all, have you used the internet to look for information on healthy lifestyle?
option_text: Once a month or less often

question: 12b. During the past 12 months, how often, if at all, have you used the internet to look for information related to anxiety, stress, or similar problems?
option_text: Once a month or less often

question: 12c. During the past 12 months, how often, if at all, have you used the internet to look for information on vaccinations?
option_text: Once a month or less often

question: 13a. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: During the past 12 months, information on the internet affected my health behaviour in a positive way.
13b. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: During the past 12 months, information on the internet helped me understand what a doctor tried to explain to me.
14a. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: The internet is useful to help people decide if their symptoms are serious enough to go to the doctor.
14b. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: The internet is useful to check that the doctor is giving people appropriate advice.
14c. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement: It is not easy to distinguish between reliable and unreliable health information on the internet.
15a. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement about vaccinations: Overall, vaccinations do more harm than good.
15b. How much do you agree or disagree with the following statement about vaccinations: It is better to develop immunity by getting ill than having a vaccination.
16a. During the past 4 weeks, how often did you have difficulties with work or household activities because of health problems?
16b. During the past 4 weeks, how often did you have bodily aches or pains?
16c. During the past 4 weeks, how often did you feel unhappy and depressed?
16d. During the past 4 weeks, how often did you lose confidence in yourself?
16e. During the past 4 weeks, how often did you feel you could not overcome your problems?
17a. During the past 12 months, how often did you visit or were visited by a doctor?
17b. During the past 12 months, how often did you visit or were visited by an [alternative/traditional/folk] health care practitioner?
18a. During the past 12 months, did it ever happen that you did not get the medical treatment you needed because you could not pay for it?
18b. During the past 12 months, did it ever happen that you did not get the medical treatment you needed because you could not take the time off work or had other commitments?
18c. During the past 12 months, did it ever happen that you did not get the medical treatment you needed because the waiting list was too long?
19. How likely is it that if you become seriously ill, you would get or not get the best treatment available in [country]?
option_text: Equal chance of getting or not getting
It's likely I would not get
It's certain I would not get

question: 20. In general, how satisfied or dissatisfied are you with the health care system in [country]?
21a. How satisfied or dissatisfied were you with the treatment you received when you last visited a doctor?
21b. How satisfied or dissatisfied were you with the treatment you received when you last visited an [alternative/traditional/folk] health care practitioner?
22. Do you smoke cigarettes, and if so about how many cigarettes a day?
option_text: Do not smoke and never did
Do not smoke now but smoked in the past
Smoke more than 40 cigarettes per day

question: 23a. How often do you drink 4 or more alcoholic drinks on the same day?
option_text: Once a month or less often

question: 23b. How often do you do physical activity for at least 20 minutes that makes you sweat or breathe more heavily than usual?
option_text: Once a month or less often

question: 23c. How often do you eat fresh fruit or vegetables?
option_text: Once a month or less often

question: 24. In general, would you say your health is …
25. Do you have a long-standing illness, a chronic condition, or a disability?
27. Do you think the [country] government should or should not have the right to do the following at times of severe epidemics? a. Shut down businesses and places of employment.
option_text: Probably should not have the right
Definitely should not have the right

question: 27b. Do you think the [country] government should or should not have the right to do the following at times of severe epidemics? b. Demand that people stay at home.
option_text: Probably should not have the right
Definitely should not have the right

question: 27c. Do you think the [country] government should or should not have the right to do the following at times of severe epidemics? c. Use digital (mobile phone) surveillance to track infected people.
option_text: Probably should not have the right
Definitely should not have the right

question: 27d. Do you think the [country] government should or should not have the right to do the following at times of severe epidemics? d. Require people to wear face masks.
option_text: Probably should not have the right
Definitely should not have the right

question: 27e. Do you think the [country] government should or should not have the right to do the following at times of severe epidemics? e. Ban public gatherings.
option_text: Probably should not have the right
Definitely should not have the right

question: 28. What kind of health insurance do you have?
option_text: National/public health insurance (including coverage by public welfare)
National/public health insurance and private/complementary insurance
Employer/union based, private/complementary and national/public health insurance

question: 29a. Do you think the [country] government should or should not have the right to do the following at times of severe epidemics? a. Place people known to carry the disease in isolation.
option_text: Probably should not have the right
Definitely should not have the right

question: 29b. Do you think the [country] government should or should not have the right to do the following at times of severe epidemics? b. Suspend compulsory education and close schools and kindergartens.
option_text: Probably should not have the right
Definitely should not have the right

question: 29c. Do you think the [country] government should or should not have the right to do the following at times of severe epidemics? c. Close borders to other countries.
option_text: Probably should not have the right
Definitely should not have the right

question: 30a. Did the way the Covid-19 pandemic was handled in [country] increase or decrease your confidence in … a. the health care system?
option_text: Neither increased it nor decreased it

question: 30b. Did the way the Covid-19 pandemic was handled in [country] increase or decrease your confidence in … b. the government?
option_text: Neither increased it nor decreased it

question: 31. Considering your work activity before the Covid-19 pandemic and at present, which of the following statements best describes your employment situation?
option_text: I did not have a job before the pandemic and I do not have a job now
I have the same job as before the pandemic
I lost my job due to the pandemic and now have a new job
I lost my job due to the pandemic and could not find a new job
I did not have a job before the pandemic and I do have a job now
I changed or left my job for reasons unrelated to the pandemic

question: 32. Thinking about the income of your household before the Covid-19 pandemic compared with now, would you say it has increased, decreased or stayed about the same?
33. Thinking about how often you met with your extended family and friends in person before the Covid-19 pandemic, would you say you now meet them in person more often, less often, or about the same?

34a. There are different opinions about people that have certain health conditions.  How much do you agree or disagree with the following statements?  Most people become very overweight because they are lazy.

34b. There are different opinions about people that have certain health conditions.  How much do you agree or disagree with the following statements?  Most people who tested positive for Covid-19 contracted the virus because they were careless.
